question: The book of Malachi is labeled as a(n) _______ (Mal 1:1)?
To whom is the book of Malachi addressed (Mal 1:1)?
What does Israel ask the Lord (Mal 1:2)?
options: Is this what you call love?

question: Who was Jacob’s brother (Mal 1:2)?
Who did the Lord say he hated (Mal 1:3)?
What land will be called a Wicked Land (Mal 1:4)?
While Edom says they will rebuild, what does the Lord say (Mal 1:4)?
Where will they say the “Great is the Lord” (Mal 1:5)?
Who does the Lord say normally receives honor (Mal 1:6)?
Who does the Lord single out as showing contempt for his name (Mal 1:6)?
What were the priests doing that the Lord points out as being wrong (Mal 1:8)?
options: By burning defiled incense on the altar
By offering blind and crippled animals as sacrifices
By not teaching the people the word of the Lord
By being drunk in the holy place

question: What did they believe their offerings would result in (Mal 1:9)?
options: For God to redeem them from their sins
For God to be gracious to them

question: What did the Lord wish someone would do (Mal 1:10)?
options: Show mercy and justice in the land

question: What did the Lord say he would not accept (Mal 1:10)?
What does the Lord say will be great among the nations (Mal 1:11)?
Where does the Lord say his name will be great (Mal 1:11)?
options: Among the priests that minister in his house

question: What do they incorrectly claim is contemptible (Mal 1:12)?
options: The sacrifices of the Most High
The food set on the Lord’s table
The blood placed in the holy place

question: What title does the Lord say he is (Mal 1:14)?
What is the proper response God calls for his name as the great king (Mal 1:14)?
Who does the Lord admonish with the promise of a curse (Mal 2:1)?
Why will God cover the priests’ faces with excrement and carry them off (Mal 2:4)?
options: So that they will never again defile his name
So that they may teach the people the way of the Lord by their example
So that they will never again offer sacrifices to the Lord
So that his covenant with Levi may continue

question: What was God’s covenant with Levi (Mal 2:5)?
options: Covenant of holiness unto the Lord

question: What did God’s covenant with Levi call for (Mal 2:5)?
What ought the lips of a priest to do (Mal 2:7)?
options: Kiss that which is holy to the Lord

question: What have the teaching of priests actually resulted in (Mal 2:8)?
options: Many to leave the straight paths
Many to defile the holy place

question: What have the priests violated (Mal 2:8)?
Malachi claims they all had one ______ (Mal 2:10)?
How has Judah broken faith (Mal 2:11)?
options: By oppressing the fatherless and widows
By marrying the daughter of a foreign god
By bringing idols into the holy place

question: Between whom is the Lord acting as a witness (Mal 2:14)?
options: You and the wife of your youth

question: Why did God make them one in flesh and spirit (Mal 2:15)?
options: He wanted to make good families

question: What does the Lord God say he hates (Mal 2:16)?
With what had the people wearied the Lord (Mal 2:17)?
Who will prepare the way before the Lord (Mal 3:1)?
Where will the Lord come suddenly (Mal 3:1)?
Who were they desiring to come who will come (Mal 3:1)?
Who will the messenger of the covenant refine like silver (Mal 3:3)?
The Lord says he will come in judgment against those who do not _____ (Mal 3:5)
In returning to the Lord what accusation does the Lord make against them (Mal 3:8)?
options: You have built idols of silver

question: How had they robbed God (Mal 3:8)?
options: In not providing food for the priests
In not following the ways of the Lord
In withholding justice from the poor

question: Why did God want them to bring their tithes into the storehouse (Mal 3:10)?
options: That the priests would have food for their families
That there may be plenty for Passover
That there may be food in his house

question: What will all nations call them because the land will be delightful (Mal 3:12)?
Why will all nations call them blessed (Mal 3:12)?
options: Because their land would be delightful
Because their fields will yield its fruit
Because peace will be within their borders
Because their kings will be righteous

question: Who did they claim escaped that said things against God (Mal 3:15)?
options: Those who turned away from God

question: What did they say that was against God (Mal 3:15)?
options: Serving God has led to a curse
God will never restore us again

question: Who was a scroll of remembrance written for (Mal 3:16)?
options: Those who are righteous and upright
Those who are just and fair
Those who feared the Lord and honor his name
Those who delight in the Lord and keep his decrees

question: What was done for those who feared the Lord and honored his name (Mal 3:16)?
options: They were given a new name in heaven
They were welcomed into the Lord’s presence with joy
A pile of stones was set up as a memorial for them
A scroll of remembrance was written in God’s presence

question: How is the coming day described (Mal 4:1)?
Who will be set on fire like stubble on that day (Mal 4:1)?
What will happen for those who revere God’s name (Mal 4:2)?
options: The sun of righteous will rise with healing in its wings
The cloud of glory will abide over them forever
The blessings of the covenant will descend on them like dew
All people will seek them to learn about the ways of the Lord

question: What does Lord tell them to remember (Mal 4:4)?
options: The law of his servant Moses
The decrees and statutes of the Lord

question: What will the coming Elijah do (Mal 4:6)?
options: Do mighty signs and wonders in the earth
Proclaim the message of the Lord
Call down rain from heaven and win favor with God and people
Turn the hearts of the father to their children
